recitedIn: At least 17 times daily in obligatory prayers
surahInfo.significance: Al-Fātiḥah is the greatest Surah in the Quran. The Prophet ﷺ said: 'The prayer is not valid for one who does not recite the Opening of the Book (Al-Fātiḥah).'

basmalah.meaning: In the name of Allah, the Most Gracious, the Most Merciful

verses.meaning: All praise is due to Allah, Lord of all the worlds
verses.explanation: We begin by praising Allah, who is the Creator and Sustainer of everything in existence.

verses.meaning: The Most Gracious, the Most Merciful
verses.explanation: Allah's mercy encompasses all of creation. Ar-Raḥmān refers to His vast mercy, Ar-Raḥīm to His special mercy for believers.

verses.meaning: Master of the Day of Judgment
verses.explanation: Allah alone owns and controls the Day when all people will be judged for their deeds.

verses.meaning: You alone we worship, and You alone we ask for help
verses.explanation: This is the core of Tawheed: directing all worship and seeking help only from Allah.

verses.meaning: Guide us to the straight path
verses.explanation: We ask Allah to keep us on the path of Islam, the path that leads to Paradise.

verses.meaning: The path of those whom You have blessed
verses.explanation: The path of the prophets, truthful ones, martyrs, and righteous people.

verses.meaning: Not of those who have earned Your anger, nor of those who have gone astray
verses.explanation: We seek refuge from the path of those who knew the truth but rejected it, and those who went astray through ignorance.
